event_blocks.summary: The Maury show segment opens with a dramatic title card and voiceover declaring 'It's an outrageous hour of DNA drama,' followed by a studio scene where a man in a black shirt and a woman in a pink top embrace, while a man in a white shirt with a gold chain looks serious. A female voice exclaims, 'My husband's not the father of your baby!' as the host, Maury, questions why a man is denying paternity. The studio audience shouts, 'You're not the father!' as a male voice describes split-screen children. Maury announces, 'We're gonna find out right now!' A woman's voice shares, 'At 16 years old, I found out I was pregnant,' with Maury adding, 'And then he said he wasn't the father.' A male voice dismissively says, 'I can't be tied down to a baby that only belonged to me.' As Maury questions, 'What are you so upset about?', a woman in a teal top reacts with shock. Another female voice defensively states, 'I never cheated on...' The atmosphere shifts to celebration as the audience cheers, 'You are the father!', and a man in a white shirt raises his arms triumphantly. The segment concludes with a man in a pinstripe shirt and a woman in a black top with a white collar making peace signs, while Maury sets up the next segment, 'Two kids denied by one man.' The 'Maury' logo is visible throughout, underscored by suspenseful and dramatic background music. Later, the Woman in green top, identified as Tyisha, passionately declares, 'Maury, this fool had audacity to call my child a bastard!' as the graphic '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?' amplifies the tension. Tyisha accuses 'Corey' of being 'a sorry, pathetic, no good excuse for a man' and blames 'Corey's new wife, Tawana' for the situation, asserting, 'My baby looks just like me and Corey.' The Woman in teal top reacts with shock, while the Man in white shirt with gold chain appears serious. Tyisha resolves, 'When the results come back that Corey is the father, it's time to face the truth.' The Man in black shirt embraces the Woman in pink top in a moment of emotional relief as the audience erupts in cheers. Maury stands on stage, gesturing intensely, concluding, 'But you see, Corey is not here alone because...' The dramatic lighting with red and orange hues underscores the intense studio talk show environment. The segment then focuses on Magan, 17, in a teal top, who is visibly distressed and emphatically denies cheating, insisting Eric is the father, challenging him with a skeptical 'Okay, you will. We'll see about that.' Eric, 18, in a pinstripe shirt and black cap with a dollar sign logo, looks concerned, responding with hesitation and assertion, 'Yeah, I will. I promise that, yeah.' Maury, in a black sweater, introduces a segment with a commanding voice, 'This is what Genie had to say. Watch.' A buzzer sounds, and the mood shifts dramatically to a black-and-white shot of a tear-streaked, anguished Woman in green top. Jeanne, Magan’s Mother, wearing glasses and a maroon top, walks down a ramp, visibly frustrated, her voice loud and forceful as she demands Eric step up as a father, calling him a 'worthless excuse for a man,' and expressing disgust that he denies her 'grandbaby Braden.' The studio audience reacts with cheers and clapping. The scene returns to Eric, who continues to speak with concern and hesitation, while Magan, still distressed, sits beside him. The Woman in green top confronts them, asking 'What are you up in his face about?' Magan, angry, accuses Eric of being a liar, stating, 'He's a liar. It's all he ever does is lie. Yeah, you are! That's all you know how to do!' Eric defensively retorts, 'Well, it's none of your business anyway!' to which Magan fiercely responds, 'It is my business! It's my daughter! And I'm supporting her, not you!' The audience claps in response to Magan's strong words, as she challenges Eric, 'What part of that do you not understand, Eric?' Eric dismissively calls the situation 'stupid.' Throughout, the on-screen graphic '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?' remains constant, anchoring the dramatic tension, with lighting shifting between warm studio tones and stark black-and-white for emotional emphasis.

Man in black shirt.description: Wearing a black shirt, shown in a split-screen with two children, suggesting he is a central figure in the DNA storyline, possibly the subject of the paternity test, and is likely 'Eric' who Maury states refuses to acknowledge paternity.

Woman in black top with white collar.description: Wearing a black top with a white collar, shown in a close-up with a serious, concerned expression, possibly reflecting on the situation.

Man in white shirt with gold chain.description: Wearing a white shirt and a prominent gold chain, his face partially shadowed, appearing serious and intense, suggesting involvement in the paternity drama, but with no direct audio in this segment.

Man in pinstripe shirt.description: Wearing a black cap with a dollar sign logo and a pinstripe shirt, identified as Eric, 18, looking concerned and defensive as he is addressed by Maury regarding the baby.

Split-screen children.description: Two young children shown side-by-side, one with curly hair and the other with a shaved head, likely the subjects of the DNA test, possibly linked to the Man in black shirt.

Man in white shirt (celebrating).description: Wearing a white shirt and dark pants, walking down a ramp, initially appearing serious. He is identified by Maury as 'Eric' and speaks with a firm voice, stating, 'Never seen this baby' and 'No, she wouldn't let me see him,' denying paternity and accusing Magan of preventing access to the child.

Studio audience.description: A group of people in the background, some clapping, others with shocked or engaged expressions, reacting to the unfolding drama.

Woman in teal top.description: Wearing a teal top, identified as Magan, 17, with a distressed expression, speaking emphatically and gesturing with her hands as she denies cheating and insists Eric is the father, responding "Yeah!" and stating, "And I supported you for a year! So don't tell me my business!"

Woman in pink top.description: Wearing a pink top, embracing the Man in black shirt, appearing emotionally connected and possibly relieved or happy.

Woman in green top.description: Wearing a green top, her face contorted in distress, speaking emphatically, later holding a baby, with dramatic lighting shifts between color and black-and-white, emphasizing emotional intensity. Her voice is confrontational and questioning as she asks, 'What are you up in his face about?' and 'He's a what?'.

Maury.description: Wearing a black sweater, standing on stage, gesturing with intensity, driving the narrative forward. He is heard asking questions, addressing Jeanne as 'Genie', and dramatically introducing the baby by name and age, stating, "When it comes to four-month-old Braden... Eric..."

Tawanna.description: A woman wearing a floral top, seated beside Corey, listening and reacting with a serious expression, identified as Corey’s wife and part of the couple disputing paternity.

Corey.description: A man wearing a light-colored shirt with a cross necklace, seated and gesturing while speaking, identified as part of the married couple Corey & Tawanna, claiming he did not father Tyisha’s baby.

Young woman with long dark hair.description: A young woman with long dark hair, shown covering her face with her hands, appearing distressed or overwhelmed, possibly reacting to a shocking DNA result.

Baby on screen.description: A close-up image of a baby’s face, wide-eyed and innocent, displayed on screen, later identified by Maury as 'four-month-old Braden'.

Jeanne (Magan’s Mother).description: Wearing glasses and a maroon top, speaking with visible frustration and anger, demanding Eric step up and be a father to her grandbaby, emphasizing he is still a 'little boy' with dialogue like, "You don't need to be talking your mouth... you're smack, you're bumping your gums a little bit too much. Maybe you need to learn and listen, you need to grow up, boy!" and "You're the one who got her pregnant!" She also states, "I buy the wipes, I buy his clothes, I buy his diapers, I buy everything that baby has! You ain't bought nothing! So what kind of a man are you?" Her name 'Genie' is also spoken by Maury.

event_blocks.summary: The Maury show segment opens with an intense focus on paternity, featuring Raymond, his fiancée Lakiesha, and Shanae, who claims Raymond is the father of her two children, Raymond Junior and Phoenix. Raymond vehemently denies paternity, asserting that DNA tests will prove his claims, while Lakiesha supports him, questioning Shanae's motives and Raymond's past relationships. Shanae counters by presenting evidence of Raymond's involvement, including alleged hospital visits and a marriage proposal, and criticizes his perceived neglect and immaturity. The studio audience reacts with a mix of shock, cheers, and gasps throughout the heated exchanges, which are amplified by dramatic lighting and rapid scene transitions. Maury guides the confrontation, probing Raymond about his denials and Lakiesha about her stance. The segment culminates with the reveal of the DNA test results for Phoenix, where Maury declares Raymond to be the father, eliciting a strong audience reaction and a demand for an apology from Lakiesha, while Raymond appears shocked and Lakiesha resigned. Earlier, the video also featured a preview of the 'MISTAKES by nosey' app with interactive story choices and a brief scene of the Man in black shirt with a Woman in pink top, and a split-screen teasing a paternity question about two children under dramatic red lighting.

The segment opens dramatically with Raymond, in a pinstripe shirt with a goatee, and his fiancée Lakiesha, wearing a red top and gold necklace, standing together as on-screen text highlights Raymond’s claim that he is not the father of Shanae’s children. Lakiesha immediately asserts in a firm voice, “That's me. Right here. Not on me. He is not the father,” as the scene cuts to Maury, in a black sweater, gesturing emphatically on stage with the show’s logo and graphic overlay '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. Maury then states, “They're determined to prove that Shanae is wrong, wrong,” as Raymond adds in his own voice, “Shanae, you know as well as I know that these kids are not mine.” The atmosphere is intense, with rapid cuts to black-and-white and sepia-toned close-ups of a wide-eyed baby, symbolizing the emotional stakes. A woman with short hair and large hoop earrings, wearing a black and white patterned top, appears, speaking with a concerned and aggressive expression, accusing Shanae, “What kind of woman has Raymond wait 15 hours for your kids when you don't even show? And you tricked me into signing that birth certificate just so you could get child support off of me.” The woman with short hair interjects again, “Raymond Jr. has red hair. He's not even as dark as my man. Don't you dare say that this is none of my business,” adding, “Any whore that takes advantage of my man becomes my business.” Raymond then questions the paternity of another child, “And as for Phoenix, we weren't even together when you got pregnant. So how do I know she's mine?” The woman with short hair delivers a final scathing remark, “You are nothing but a lying, childish, poor excuse for a mother. Quit calling and leave my man alone,” prompting Maury to react with “How about that?” and “What do you think?” The segment uses quick transitions, split screens, and dramatic zooms, with alternating warm red and stark monochrome lighting, heightening the tension around the DNA test results and the couple’s relationship under scrutiny, all underscored by audience reactions and dramatic music.

Split-screen visuals of two young children, one with curly hair and one with a shaved head, are shown alongside Raymond’s face, reinforcing the central question of paternity.

The scene ends with Raymond and Lakiesha being escorted off the stage by staff, as the audience continues to react to Raymond's repeated denial, highlighting the high-stakes drama of the paternity reveal.

event_blocks.summary: The segment opens with Maury, in a black sweater, on stage with a clipboard labeled 'DBP' and the on-screen text '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?', introducing a paternity segment. After a dramatic buzzer sound and a split-screen showing a man in a black shirt with two children, Maury reveals, "Raymond, you are not the father!" eliciting gasps from the studio audience. As the man in black covers his face, a woman screams, "Oh Raymond!" and another shouts, "He said she cheated!" Maury clarifies, "He's the father of that little girl, okay?" to which a woman responds, "I can't believe he's the father of either one of them, it's right!" Maury concludes, "Well, he's the father of one," followed by another buzzer and "Not the father!" A narrator introduces Shanae, who claims a unique reason for paternity, and the scene shifts to a hallway with Eric, a man in a pinstripe shirt, and a woman in a red top. Shanae, with short hair and hoop earrings, explains her son Gabriel was born with birth defects requiring surgery, and Maury adds tension by mentioning cheating accusations and a "free ride." A photo of a sick baby appears with the text 'A SICK CHILD DENIED!'. The segment then focuses on Michael, in a blue and white striped shirt, and Anna, in a pink top, with Anna passionately declaring her fury at Michael for not being there for their son Gabriel, who was born with birth defects. Michael, with a stern expression, believes a DNA test will prove he is not the father, accusing Anna of cheating the night of conception and wanting his money. Anna vehemently denies cheating, stating she is a million percent sure Michael is the father and wants to work things out to raise their son as a family. The studio audience reacts with shock and applause. The segment culminates with Maury announcing, 'When it comes to nine-month-old Gabriel, Michael, you are the father!' The audience erupts as Anna reacts with shock and anger, while Michael exclaims, 'I told you! I told you!' A photo of a newborn in a hospital and a baby in a blue bouncer are shown. The segment ends with Anna confronting Michael, who remains defiant, as Maury suggests they 'work things out.'

Upbeat, dramatic music plays as a voiceover declares, 'You are the father! You're busted!', followed by Maury's intense narration, 'The results are unforgettable. And the results are in.' Maury recounts Anna's story with dramatic pauses, explaining how she met Michael, and how two weeks after their baby, Gabriel, was born, two shocking things happened: doctors discovered Gabriel had birth defects requiring life-threatening surgery, eliciting gasps from the studio audience, and Michael then accused Anna of cheating, denying he was the 'sick baby's father.' The studio audience is visible, reacting with shock and gasps to Maury's revelations. The lighting is dramatic with red and orange hues, and the Maury show logo is consistently visible. The segment builds tension with rapid cuts between Maury, Anna, and the baby images, culminating in a close-up of Anna speaking emotionally, her face reflecting pain and accusation, as Maury concludes, 'This is Anna's story.'

The segment opens with a distressed woman in a red top, later identified as Anna, passionately declaring, "I am furious at Michael," as an on-screen graphic poses the question, '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. Anna recounts their two-year relationship, their plans for marriage and a baby, and Michael's accusations of cheating. Visually, the scene intercuts between Anna's emotional testimony, a woman in a beige top holding a baby, a pensive man in a blue and white striped shirt (Michael), a baby in a blue bouncer, and a photo of a newborn in a hospital setting. Anna's anguish intensifies as she reveals, "What really broke my heart was what happened the day that Gabriel was born. He said that he didn't even think he was the father." She continues, her voice filled with pain, explaining that "My little Gabriel was born with birth defects. And the doctor said that if he didn't have the surgery, then he would die," questioning, "How could Michael not a baby that needs him so much?" She vows, "Today, I'm gonna prove that Michael is the father, so he can step up and be a dad to our miracle baby." The studio audience responds with applause, as Maury, in a black sweater, gestures emphatically on stage. Maury then addresses Anna, confirming, "Gabriel came through that surgery, he's great. There's no problem, right?" to which Anna replies, "He's doing fine." Maury presses, "Why are you so angry at that man?" and Anna, now in a pink top, urgently states, "I am sick and tired of his [bleep]. I am tired of him not being there for his son." Michael, shown with a stern expression and a lower-third graphic stating his belief that a DNA test will prove he is not the father, remains silent. The segment concludes with a close-up of Gabriel's wide-eyed, innocent face displayed on screen, underscoring the emotional stakes, while dramatic lighting shifts heighten the tension.

The segment opens in a tense, dramatically lit studio with red and orange hues, where Michael, in a blue and white striped shirt, sits with arms crossed, displaying a stern, defensive expression. An on-screen graphic declares '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?' setting the stage for a paternity drama. The camera cuts to Anna, in a pink top, her face contorted in distress and accusation as she emotionally states, "I'm tired of him promising to come see the baby. I have kept the baby up late past bedtime, waiting for him to come see him... He promises to come see him, he shows up at 1 o'clock in the morning. She continues, her voice rising in frustration, "I even let my baby go stay at a family member of his house, so he could spend the night with his father. He didn't even come home! My baby was there for two days and he never come home to see him. He stayed at a different family member's house." Maury, the host, in a black sweater, gestures emphatically as he interjects, "Because he says you were cheating." Anna, with tearful eyes, vehemently denies, "I never cheated on him. Brief cuts show a calm baby in a blue bouncer sucking on a toy, and a photo of a newborn in a hospital setting with medical tubes, underscoring the emotional stakes. The segment ends with Anna’s face in close-up, her eyes wide with pain, as her emotional plea lingers.

The video opens with a close-up of a woman in a purple top, identified as Jessica, speaking with a serious expression, her braided hair and large hoop earrings visible, as the on-screen graphic reads '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. A woman's emphatic voice, identified as Janette, immediately declares, "Jessica, I'm a trillion percent sure that my brother is not your son's father." The scene cuts to a man in a black shirt, shown in a split-screen with two children, suggesting he is a central figure in the paternity storyline. A man's angry voice, identified as Ernest, then accuses Jessica, stating, "Jessica, when I let you move in with me, I know you were sleeping with one of my friends. And I know one of them might be the father." The video then transitions to a man in a white shirt with a gold chain, his face partially shadowed, appearing intense and serious, as Ernest's voice continues, "Back in the days, I didn't think how dirty you was 'cause I was in love with you. But when I found out you had four kids with four different fathers, I knew you was a ho." A woman in a green top is shown with a distressed expression, speaking emphatically, later holding a baby, with dramatic lighting shifts, while Janette's voice returns, asserting, "I'm going to protect my brother from a nasty, trifling bitch like you." Ernest's voice continues his accusations, "You stay out until 5 o'clock in the morning. And I know you was creeping 'cause that's what hoes do." The studio audience is shown reacting with a collective "Oh!" and then applause, adding to the emotional weight. Maury is then heard asking, "What do you think of that?" A woman's defensive and agitated voice, identified as Jessica, shouts, "She wasn't there when I slept with him. She was not there. I'm a hundred percent sure that he's not the father! And what, what did you say? What? What is it? What is it?" The segment concludes with the intense verbal exchange.

The Maury show opens dramatically with red and purple lighting, featuring the on-screen graphic '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. The studio audience immediately shouts, "Please tell me! Do they look alike? Please tell me!", to which Jessica, a woman in a purple top with braided hair and large hoop earrings, reacts with "Oh, what? What?". Maury, in a black sweater, stands on stage with expressive hand gestures, addressing Jessica and stating, "They look alike." Jessica passionately agrees, "Yes! He looks just like his sister!", and confirms to Maury that she and Ernest were together for "like two years," having known him for "eight years." A lower-third graphic and Maury's voice confirm that Ernest's sister, Janetta, made the call for the DNA test. Jessica, with a distressed face, then shockingly reveals, "She was like sleeping with us. She was way in New York while me and him was in VA," before aggressively confronting someone, likely Janetta or one of the men, with expletives, shouting, "You need to get a reality check, bitch! You're a bitch! You're a bitch! You're a hoe!" The audience cheers and claps loudly in response. Maury repeatedly tries to interject, calling her name and even echoing her exclamations. Jessica then states, "Me and my sister was close," and confidently declares, "They gonna apologize when this paternity test is seen." A man's voice from the stage interjects, "You're lying, man," to which Jessica fiercely retorts, "Yeah, you're lying, be a man!" The camera alternates between close-ups of Jessica and the two tense, serious men on a split-screen – one in a red 'Brooklyn' shirt and the other in a black jacket – as Maury continues to try and manage the escalating confrontation.

The segment opens with a close-up of Jessica, the Woman in purple top with braided hair and large hoop earrings, speaking passionately as the on-screen graphic reads '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. Maury's voice asks her, "Did Ernest say to you he would never he would always be there for you and your son?" Jessica emotionally responds, "Yes, he said that he would never leave me and my son. He loved us forever." The scene cuts to a split-screen showing the Man in black shirt with two children, reinforcing his central role. Dramatic, suspenseful music builds as Maury announces, "We're going to bring him out here. All right? Ernest his. Here they are." The studio audience erupts in cheers and shouts of "Whoa!" as the Man in red shirt (Ernest, wearing a 'Brooklyn' shirt and a cap) and the Man in black jacket enter the stage, both appearing tense. Ernest gestures emphatically, denying paternity, stating, "That baby don't look like me first of all. You see that mole on his head? None of these people in my family got no mole on their head. That look like somebody that was in the street at my crib. Get out of here. He ain't my son. Get out of here." He then aggressively calls Jessica "You a hoe, man." The Man in black jacket reacts with raised arms amidst the audience's shocked and engaged reactions. Maury, in a black sweater, stands on stage with expressive hand gestures, driving the narrative. The on-screen text updates to 'ERNEST SAYS THE DNA TEST WILL PROVE HE IS NOT THE FATHER OF JESSICA'S SON', underscoring the central conflict. Jessica continues to speak with urgency, while the two men face off on the brightly lit, theatrical studio stage, enhancing the drama of the paternity dispute.

The segment opens with Maury, wearing a black sweater, standing on stage with an expressive, animated demeanor, introducing a dramatic paternity case with the on-screen graphic '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. Maury sets the stage, stating, "that you would be there for that child because you've seen all these guys never wanted to pony up to take care of these kids." The scene cuts to a woman in a purple top, identified as Jessica, who speaks passionately with braided hair and large hoop earrings, her expression intense as she recounts her story. The camera then shows a man in a red shirt with 'Brooklyn' written in white script and a white cap with red trim, identified as Ernest, seated beside a woman in a black jacket over a white shirt, identified as Janetta, who reacts with shock and gestures emphatically. Ernest, appearing tense, interjects, "Well, that was until I found out what type of girl she was," while Janetta loudly accuses Jessica, "Because she had four kids by four different men!" Ernest, agitated, adds, "You ain't telling nobody, you ain't tell me nobody." Maury then directly asks Janetta, "Why are you in this, Janetta? Why?", to which she responds, "Because this my little brother and I'm going to back him up from any little thing like that." Ernest, still emotional, shouts, "You don't know nothing, man. They ain't my baby! Get out of here!" as Janetta tries to calm him, saying "Sit down!" and echoing his sentiment, "Super salty!" The on-screen text reveals that Ernest says Jessica never told him she had four kids by four other men. Maury builds suspense, stating, "We're gonna find out right now," as the scene shifts to a wider shot of the studio, where Ernest stands up and walks toward the stage, arms raised in celebration, shouting "Oh, yeah!" as the audience erupts in cheers and dramatic music swells. The text overlay announces, 'THE RESULTS ARE IN!', and Ernest is shown joyfully jumping and dancing, while Janetta claps and smiles. The audience is visibly engaged, with some clapping and others covering their mouths in surprise. The final shot shows Ernest celebrating on stage, with the text 'ERNEST IS ABOUT TO FIND OUT IF HE IS THE FATHER OF JESSICA'S SON, ERNEST JR.' appearing, indicating the emotional climax of the reveal.

The video opens with a tense studio scene from the Maury show, where Jessica, a woman in a purple top with braided hair and large hoop earrings, speaks passionately and gestures emphatically, stating, "He was being my baby daddy, my baby daddy, my baby daddy." Maury then calls out, "Janetta?" A lower-third graphic reads '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. Behind her, a man in a black shirt stands near a screen displaying a baby’s face. The camera cuts to a man in a red 'Brooklyn' shirt and another in a black jacket, both standing beside a seated man in a black jacket and cap, who raises his hand dramatically. Jessica interjects, "Yes, and I want him to go apologize to him and kiss him on his feet," as the studio audience reacts with shock and excitement, some clapping, others covering their mouths, and cheering loudly. This is followed by a dramatic red-tinted transition with text 'IS HIS SISTER RIGHT OR WRONG?' and 'YOU WON’T BELIEVE WHAT SHE SAID!', while Maury's voice states, "Chenae swears that no one else could be the father, and you won't believe the reason why." The video then cuts to a black screen with the word 'OR' and a phone interface showing a 'MISTAKES by nosey' app, accompanied by upbeat, dramatic music, and a voice declaring, "You are the father. Your mistake." This is followed by a red-lit studio shot of the audience cheering. The segment ends with a dynamic graphic overlay of '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?' in bold red and white text, as Maury's voice begins to say, "The results are un..." before being cut off.

event_blocks.summary: Maury, clad in a black sweater, strides across the studio stage amidst audience applause and cheers, as the on-screen text '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?' introduces the segment. Ernest, wearing a red shirt with 'Brooklyn' scripted in white, voices his disbelief, exclaiming, "Oh my god, man, I don't believe this, man," to which Maury responds, "And you'll be a... you'll be a good dad." The scene transitions to a red-lit hallway where Ernest stands tensely next to a Man in a black jacket. A lower-third graphic confirms, 'THE DNA TEST PROVED ERNEST IS THE FATHER OF JESSICA'S SON, ERNEST JR.' Ernest then offers an apology, stating, "I'm sorry, we actually just stuff that she didn't do. I'm just sorry," followed by Jessica, the Woman in a black and white patterned top with large hoop earrings, also apologizing, "Jessica, I'm sorry. I'm sorry. I am, I'm sorry." The emotional resolution unfolds as Ernest cradles and kisses a baby, identified by Jessica as "Jojo," dressed in a striped onesie, while she affectionately says, "I miss you, baby. Hi Jojo." The video cuts back to Jessica, her expression serious and concerned, accompanied by the text 'YOU WON'T BELIEVE WHAT SHE SAID!', as Maury's voiceover previews another segment: "Shenae swore that no one else could be the father, and you won't believe the reason why." Dramatic music swells as Maury reappears on stage, followed by a graphic displaying three faces—a smiling child, a man with a goatee, and a young child—alongside DNA test text. The segment concludes with Jessica speaking with intense emotion, as the show's branding 'after THE SHOW' appears, and Maury's voice declares, "The results are unforgettable. And the results are in," before introducing a new case involving "Shenae," "Raymond," and "Laquisha" concerning "two kids." The lighting shifts dramatically between warm studio tones and intense reds, underscoring the emotional gravity of the paternity revelations.

event_blocks.summary: In a studio setting, Maury, dressed in a black sweater, dramatically narrates a paternity drama, highlighted by a graphic stating '6 BABIES...6 DNA TESTS...WHICH MAN IS THE DAD?'. He declares, "Look at them, they look just like him," and reveals that while one paternity test proved a man fathered a daughter, a second DNA test caused shockwaves, leading him to exclaim, "You are not the father!" to the studio audience's cheers, gasps, screams, and applause. A man in a black shirt stands near a screen, and a woman in a black and white patterned top with large hoop earrings appears concerned. The scene shifts to a close-up of a man in a striped shirt and a woman in a red top. Backstage, the woman in the black and white patterned top urgently speaks with an intensifying expression, with lighting alternating between color and black-and-white. Executive Producer Paul Follhaber is heard reassuring an emotional Shanay, who insists, "He's the only guy I slept with. I don't even sleep with guys... I saw the test myself," to which Paul responds, "It's okay. Listen, you're a good mom." Maury concludes the segment, "We hope that when Shanay gets home, she can find the answers she's looking for. Until next time, America." Credits roll over scenes of a woman in a green top holding a baby, a woman in a teal top gesturing, and a man in a pinstripe shirt speaking, all accompanied by upbeat music, before the show's logo and copyright appear with the visibly shocked studio audience.
